channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: cardano, ADA is imploding right now — 46 replies — 184.0/hr

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-05-28 14:42 UTC

cardano, ADA
is imploding right now

62275698
the whole space is fucking dying. sad that it all ended up this way

62276473

Imagine buying cardanus, ever! This shit has less than 500$ stables liquidity onchain.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62278173

62278448
>>62277437
>Blockchain tech is actually not that useful

https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/may/12/dtcc-collaborates-with-chainlink-to-advance-24-7-collateral-management

62278452
What kind of retard do you have to be to be interested in Cardano? Same as polkadot. Jesuschrist, it's a blockchain and they make it out to be like if it was a gothic cathedral.

Hyperliquid is less than 2 years old and is already the most used blockchain.

It was obvious it was all bullshit.

62278457
>>62278452
hyperliquid is closer to a private blockchain. it only has 27 validators.

62278477
>>62276491
This. A major kike stakeholder in a financial scam - never seen before.
In general, I'll never trust a crypto or financial project with a kike or a software project with jeets.

62278491
>>62278477
is Charles a jew? i always assumed he was a goy with all the western shit
>>62277725
i don't know, i sold all my ada in 2021.. but basically charles been scamming the treasury or something for years, makes sense, not surprised.. recently japanese validators or whatever voted to end funding the ""research"" laps funded by cardano.. charles apperently has always gone apeshit and burned every bridge but now it's become exponential bridge burnings and blockings..
ADA is a scam... nothing new.. but finally seeing Charles flame out in real time is kinda neat

62278891
>>62278491

62279915
The cope in this thread is unreal. Cry more, kiddies.

62280492
The narrative haven't really been evolving. Haven't seen your threads for some time and this is the buy signal I honestly was looking and not hoping for. Because the hate your try to project in inconsequential to you.

62280512
I'm not in ADA since 2021.
But now, i am daily scammed by BTC.
Dunno what's worse (financially) desu.

62280533
>>62277725
Some cheap twitter beef causing uproar. It's still one of the most technologically sound chains out there, but some people tried a power grab the minute Charles burnt his keys.
So the only mistake that Charles has made there is that he had too much good faith in those that are stabbing him in the back now. I guess that's what you get if you don't know who your real friends are.

62280595
jeets are still buying crypto lmao
lmfao even

62280622
>>62278448

Call me back when stable coins make up less than 99% of all “tokenized real world assets” then maybe I’ll change my mind. Until then I think you’re delusional if you think it’s useful or “mass adoption” to have a handful of turbonerd billionaires and hedge funds use USDC or UST in place of traditional clearinghouses

62280637
>>62277444

62280714

62280809
>>62278477

62280833

62281020

so in a way, you're like a jeet

62281159
I never trusted Charles so I never bought cardano…dude is an obvious psycho

62281608

62281639
>>62275591

62281653

If you still made the decision to put money in this project, its your fault, really

62282732

Wow that's very bold and stupid. Homeboy thought he could just jump into an industry as complex as healthcare. "Mayo Clinic of the West" lmao

62282767

62282801
>>62280714
No they aren't.

62282929
>>62282801

62284091
>>62275698

buy signal

62284100
>>62281020
>crypto is banned in india
just googled it and you're full of shit

62284157
>>62277590
>I remember you could play Super Mario 64 on the Internet Computer Protocol (bing bing wahoo on ICP, jej) but who the fuck cares?
The whole point of that experiment killed ICP the moment it was taken off. What's the point of your web infrastructure if regular web rules still apply to it? Why the fuck is your product worth dick if you're just reinventing the wheel?
>buh muh token costs!!
Irrelevant. If price to run the grid increases because token become more valuable, then whole economic incentive to migrate to your web infrastructure falls apart.

item.title: Jannies, excuse me while I KILL MYSELF — 25 replies — 52.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Jannies, excuse me while I KILL MYSELF

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-05-28 14:27 UTC

OP

It's fucked for real this time. completely dead. AI bubble will pop, S&P will collapse and drag BTC even lower, at a certain point Saylor will get liquidated. Tom lee will get liquidated. BTC at $15k and ETH at $200 is not unrealistic. Short all crypto. I've been in this space for a decade. It's truly dead. Saylor will be sucking cock in hell wondering where it all went wrong. Tom Lee will shove a pineapple up his own ass. We are FUCKED. IT IS FUCKING OVER. CRYPTO IS DEAD. THE CLARITY ACT WILL NEVER PASS. BLACKROCK WILL DIVEST. BINANCE WILL SHUT DOWN. WE ARE ALL FUCKED AND GOING TO SUCK COCK IN HELL. VITALIK WILL BE PERFORMING FELATIO ON ORTHODOX JEWISH RABBI IN HELL. WE BOUGHT A FUCKING PET ROCK FAKE PIECE OF FAGGOT SHIT ABANDON SHIP NOW NOW !!!!!!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62281675
whoopsie poopsie xD

62281676
????????????

62281679
Better luck next time

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62281685
>>62281679
Oh my FUCKING GOD

62281691
JUST IN TIME FOR CHAINLINKS 7 YEAR ANIVERSARY ON MAINNET AND THE RELEASE OF INEFERNO TO BOOT LMAOOO

62281698
>>62281668
Should've bought HBAR...!

62281705

Yeah you’re fucked buddy

62281711

62281713

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=74NluS3jzTo

brb meds

62281742
OP here. I'm back. I'm more relaxed and thinking logically now. Realistically they are going to begin having military style tribunals for crypto advocates. Arthur hayes will be buck broken on CSPAN. Tom lee will shove a pineapple up his own ass. Satoshi nakatoto will be finally revealed and be drawn.and quartered on national television (in israel for some reason) we are so fucked. It is so over. (Not reading replies)

62281754
>>62281668
ive been in crypto since 2016

you are an emotional pussy nothing will happen or ever happen stfu

62281776
>>62281754
Fuck you homo. Tom Lee pineapple prepping faggot

62281782
>>62281776

62281789

>new tech fad emerges
>tech giants become even more profitable than before
>spx goes up nonstop forever
>crypto remains a pump and dump scamcoin forever

62281798

Your father is a FAGGOT and your mother FUCKS NIGGERS

62281831
>>62281798
Alright this is true

62281862
>>62281742
no crying in the nigger casino

62281983
>>62281776
no fuck you nigger god u guys just cry nonstop fuck u fuck u

62282258
We're all gonna burn in brimstone niggerhell for our investments

62282479
It’s over.

62282734
>>62281862
Bitcoin people are deluded. Some retarded fuck said that btc would be the reserve currency of the world. Can you imagine if that actually happened? Imagine a huge crisis happened, and you needed to dilute the chaos by issuing some money to the struggling businesses. What are you gonna do lmao? forcibly divide everyone's bitcoins up and move the price up? every lost bitcoin is a lost unit of "reserve currency" btw

DONT forget to livestream it faggot

62283517

item.title: Honestly owning rentals made me realize how helpless alot of adults are now. — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Honestly owning rentals made me realize how helpless alot of adults are now.

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-28 14:48 UTC

OP

Honestly owning rentals made me realize how helpless alot of adults are now.

I bought a bunch of duplexes down south back around 2018-2020 because the numbers made sense. Cheap property taxes, cheap insurance, cheap properties in general. One duplex I paid $124k for is bringing in around $1925/mo total now and the tennants renewed instantly after the last increase.

Had a tennant last week who submitted a maintenence request because the microwave “stopped heating.” Property manager drove over there and the door wasnt shut all the way.

Another guy called because his screen door came off the track. PM slid it back in place in about 10 seconds and the guy goes “much obliged sir” like he just repaired farm equipment from the 1800s or something.

Whats weird is the older tennants almost never bother anybody. Pay on time. Maybe ask for a sink fix every few years. Younger ones send paragraphs over tiny stuff.

Not even trying to sound mean but I geniunely think people are less capable now than they were 20 years ago.

People online act like owning rentals is some impossible genius level thing but honestly half the job is just dealing with grown adults who dont know why the wifi stopped working after they unplugged the router to move furniture around.

Early replies (first 3)

62283853
>>62283816
It never sounds worth the money

62283860
How much of your revenue do you devote to hookers

62283871
>>62283816
I will buy one of these when I make it from Link. Probably in a college town. I won’t care about any profits but negotiate BJ’s for rent if they are unable to pay

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283878
>>62283816

Like this post, what exactly do you contribute?

62283893
>paying a property manager when you're only making 2k a month
i got sick of landlording. Every single time a tentant text me I was filled with dread and could never be bothered doing anything about the problems. You pay a shit load of taxes on it here too, not worth

62283964
>>62283816
>Live in a disposable consumerist society
>shocked when people don't know how to fix shit
Only reason I learned to fix shit was because I was too poor to replace. Now that I'm doing better, I still fix my shit because my money came from blood sweat and tears and I'll be damned if it goes to some fucking company so a CEO can take in more profit and his kids can go to Europe on a whim

62284183
>>62283816
so what you're telling me is owning a rental is like running daycare
yeah that sounds shit

LINK: -5%

This crypto market is just absolutely retarded.
Probably why it's up ass creek while stocks are soaring at ATH.

62283791
>>62283765
People should know that the same announcement means different things for cryptos

62283805
>>62283743
Chainlink is like the white kid that everyone expects to do well so it's no suprise when it gets "good news". Every other altcoin is like a nigger, people will be euphoric on just about any news.

62283861
I thought narrative was that market stopped caring about announcements? I heard that like 500 times from fuddies

62283874
>>62283743
My loomen be boomen

62283928
>>62283787
its chainlink suppressing its own token, it has nothing to do with the market being stupid.

62283932
>>62283928

why would Chainlink dump BTC to suppress Link?

62283944
>>62283932
because a bunch of far right peasants bought in too early

62284025
>>62283743
how many 10%, 30%, 50% or even 100% green candles have been suppressed from linkies thus far?

item.title: Question for oldfags about vidya prices: — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Question for oldfags about vidya prices:

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-28 14:49 UTC

OP

i looked into the price of SNES games as well as master system back in the days:

-average snes game sold for 50$ in the early 9às, inflation adjusted that's 120$ today
Some sold higher than that : donkey kong came for 70$, so 150$ today

-30 to 60$ for sega master system, but it was the late 80s so like 130$ today.

Why are gooners crying about the price of their vidyas? Things used to be way more expensive for a 2 d platformer that would give you max 100 hours of play if not half. Also today's collector price for most of those games is still LOWER than what people paid for when it came out brand new back in the days, so still no profit after holding for 30+ years kek

Early replies (first 3)

62283131
>>62283119
There was a time not so long ago when you could hardly give that shit away. Nobody wanted to play old 2D games when there was amazing new stuff coming out that you couldn't have even imagined a few years prior.

62283133
>>62283119
Oldfag here, they’re crying because all these games were $1 at your local flea market in 2005, AND THEY DIDN’T EVEN WANT TO BUY. It’s even worse than missing bitcoin because it was right under their nose, they saw, they knew, and they shrugged it off.

62283135
>>62283131
snes games were cheap like 10 + years ago. Master system is still very cheap, but nobody gives a shit. I guess rn the money is in xbox / game cube games

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283136
Also, thread's theme:

https://youtu.be/E5_DDRkH70E?t=458

62284191
So as a former soiboi with a massive collection of everything from
>atari
>nes
>snes
>genesis/32x
>n64
>gc
>ps1
>dc
What am I in for and also when/how do I sell to get max value for all this shit that tbqhfam I'm just as comfy emulating? Good crt monitors are like digging for dinosaur bones and composite input screens are like asking about where the bluray players are these days. This shit is collecting dust and I've got projects that need funding

OP

62278137

Last time everyone was dunking on eth it rallied more than 100%. I might have to buy some leveraged eth etf even though I think it's a shitcoin.

62278146

It would be better if ETH costed $20. To win and become the future of finance, smart contracts need to be cheap.

62278150

62278157
>>62278052
I wont be able to buy under 1500.....

62278243

a glorified erc fully dependent on eth?

62278251

62278354
>>62278125
>only when they interviewed sergey did they even realise what chainlink was after like 6 fucking years
This says more about how irrelevant chainlink is tbcqh

62278362
I still don't know what the hell bankless is and why is everyone losing their marbles about it

62278371
>>62278052
is it time for alt season

62278387
>>62278052
they will never deliver. they never intended to

62278411
>>62278362
Only thing you should know how to sell your ether below 1500$!

62278689
Hmm with the 10+ thread saying it's over for ETH, tells me ETH season's loading. Always do the opposite of what /biz says anon

62278830
>>62278137
Everyone in crypto has been dunking on ETH since at least 2024 at this point non-stop

62278841
>>62278354
That they realised how important it is?

62279102
>>62278052
He wouldn't have had this problem of "I don't know what ETH is doing with their lives so I'm out" if he genuinely went Bankless.

62280792
>>62278841
So important that everything still operates smoothly without it. I think the only importance that was noticed was a quick dump on baggies to be had after airing.

62280817
>>62278052
Kinda grim how it seems the only top alt that people are still positive on is hype (not shilling I don't hold any)

62280838
Swap ETH for XMR, save what value you have before it goes to 0

62283515
>>62278052
people act like this is a big deal but he can buy back in at any time. in the article he says that he's rotating his capital to higher opportunity

62283540
>>62278052
(((David Hoffman)))
never trust a jew, ever.

62283587
bullish

62283647
>>62278125
they were just being polite since he was a guest, they know exactly what $link is and how it has nothing to do with CLL

item.title: BTC is not the safe investment you think it is — 10 replies — 6.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC is not the safe investment you think it is

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-28 13:18 UTC

OP

It was artificially crippled by limiting its throughput capability. The technical means of this is the limitation of the block size, by comparison harddrive sizes and internet speeds have increased between 15 and 50 times since 2010. For specialised companies laying their own cables it could be even more (a blockchain mining business might do that to improve their chances of winning a block and thereby earning more coins -> more revenue/profit).

Reasons for the limit could be stupidity or corruption, for example via Epstein funding certain Bitcoin developers: "are you still playing in the bitcoin space.? where are you, i have Andy Back on my island this weekend". When you send coins via Lightning it isn't your exact coins that arrive, instead someone else's coins arrive in the same quantity, this small difference is useful when you want to break the law).

As a result BTC is not a very safe investment. It is more similar to the emperror wearing no clothes than anything else. At some point people will figure it out as they try to solve technical challenges and realise they can't beat existing systems like VISA or Mastercard neither in terms of throughput (how many customers can actually use the system) nor in cost (how many customers can afford to use it). For example currently VISA can currently serve about nine hundred times as many customers as BTC.

For the time being however people still believe and the volatility means opportunity to make money. Just be aware of the risk that at some point the music will stop and you don't want to be left behind with no chair.

Early replies (first 3)

62278959
>>62278947
>BTC is
cool and orange buy now you tranny unc

62278997
>>62278947
saylor is gonna fk btc for afew years, hes literally running a ponzi on it (11.5% divvy) and hoping for the best

62279038
>>62278947
>Bitcoin is cool enough to work even on not super fast computer, and allow miners not on super fast connections (each second of latency matters)
BASED!
This is why Bitcoin is the king.
Alters will seethe.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62279130
>>62278947
bcash, the centralized scam lost, keep seething

62280024
>>62279038
Netflix works on a shitty computer because the shitty computer user is not running the system, he is simply a user. Netflix uses VERY strong server infrastructure.

This is true for all big sophisticated services.

SN said and I quote: "The design supports letting users just be users."

Letting BTC be run by shitty computers on purpose is something only a moron, useful idiot or scammer would come up with.

62280071
>>62278947
>>62280024
We’re getting close to 20 years of crypto and bitcoin in general and not a single “technologically superior” blockchain has displaced it despite there being thousands of coins with faster transaction times, greater volumes, etc. You can do it right now in fact, this afternoon. Even if you have no experience, you can hop onto Claude and in half a day create and deploy a blockchain that is technically superior to Bitcoin in every way. Let us know how it goes.
At this point if you’re still arguing about what Bitcoin was “meant to be” instead of what it is, you are retarded and also likely broke.

62280097
>>62280024

>Letting BTC be used by everyone at home on his own computer - is something only a moron, useful idiot or scammer would come up with!!!!!!!
>You will own no means to verify your money, and you will be happy!!!

62281152
>>62280071
I guess giving up must be the nature of the simpler minded.
>>62280097
You will be Jack Dorsey's IOU system's bitch, congrats.

62281700
Post short brokie

62283747
>>62281700
>short a super volatile asset riddled with exchange scam wicks
get tested retard

item.title: How can the US' average historical stock market return be 7%, when average historical GDP growth is 3%? — 16 replies — 5.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
How can the US' average historical stock market return be 7%, when average historical GDP growth is 3%?

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-05-28 12:01 UTC

OP

How can the US' average historical stock market return be 7%, when average historical GDP growth is 3%?

Early replies (first 3)

62275614
>>62275600
Price inflation. Greater fool theory.

62275626
>>62275600
imagine believing their posted numbers without running the equations yourself

62275702
>>62275600
All the jews calculate this already goy it's nothing to worry about

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62276224
>>62275614
These numbers are supposed to be adjusted for inflation

62280513
>>62275600
GDP isn't what you think it is. that's how. if assets are not sold or transferred, but grow in value, they are not calculated in the GDP formula. GDP is a measurement of currency flow. that's it. it's not a measurement of anything else.

to simplify: if i send you 20 USD, and you sent me 20 USD back, our GDP would be 40 USD. if i held an asset at 20k USD, and didn't sell or transfer funds, my GDP is zero.

62280524
>>62280513
So the stock market is even faker than the fake GDP?

62280556
>>62280524
as fake as our currency is, yes. when the money is fake: the jobs are fake, the markets are fake, the entertainment is fake. you need to play the game.

62280581
>>62275600
GDP is linear Stock Market is exponential

62280599
>>62275600
1. stock market doesn't represent the entire economy
2. inflationary surplus money has to go somewhere -> stocks

62280739
>>62275600

that's a trick question.

The stock market and the GDP aren't directly related.

The stock markets success isn't the only factor in determining the growth of the GDP.

That's like in baseball where a team can get a lot of hits and get on base a lot and still not necessarily score and get many runs.

62282832
>>62275600
>Capital in the Twenty-First Century (French: Le Capital au XXIe siècle) is a book written by French economist Thomas Piketty. It focuses on wealth and income inequality in Europe and the United States since the 18th century. It was first published in French (as Le Capital au XXIe siècle) in August 2013; an English translation by Arthur Goldhammer followed in April 2014.

>The book's central thesis is that when the rate of return on capital (r) is greater than the rate of economic growth (g) over the long term, the result is concentration of wealth, and this unequal distribution of wealth causes social and economic instability. Piketty proposes a global system of progressive wealth taxes to help reduce inequality and avoid the vast majority of wealth coming under the control of a tiny minority.

62282964
>>62282832
Funny how their "solutions" to this problem always involve increasing government power, in this case by stealing asset value, rather than to ever question the debasement of currency that is the actual cause of the problem. The vast majority of people with a £1m net worth who would fall under his planned Corbyn wealth tax are just old people who own a home in the south of England. Unlike billionaires they can't just move billions in assets in an instant to another jurisdiction.

62283457
>>62280513
GDP is supposed to be a measure of value-add, not mere currency flow.

62283463
>>62280599
The numbers are supposed to be adjusted for inflation

62283467
>>62276224
>he actually believes the officials numbers
You're no different than the naïve peasants in the Soviet Union, lmao

NIG NIGGER
NIGGER NIGGER NIG GUH GUH

62282417
>>62282404
A Rucka Rucka fan I see

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283795
>>62282314

62283897

its dumping since forever and op scamming clueless guys into that scam!

item.title: is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf — 123 replies — 148.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Open thread • Replies: 123 • Updated: 2026-05-28 14:06 UTC

OP

is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Early replies (first 3)

62275893
>>62275877
Checked and ikd but it's telling how Chris/fishy/juels et al are ignoring CLPR like their lifes depend on it.

62275918
>>62275893
>CLPR
Is this the new talking point? A acronym we have never heard of is another chainlink killer?

62275930
>>62275918
yes its very 'organically' being spammed across this board right now in every thread, not being shilled directly because they know that doesn't work here (they tried one thread and got no traction). hedera shills are the least organic in the space, likely because they're mostly bought/paid for, another anon saved screen caps of their payout program for the shills. also the leemon face memes are awful, just being ugly is not enough to create a meme you fucking jeets.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62281185
>>62281161
>why are you so sure that CLPR will not be adopted at the validator level?
The fact that every chain has to alter their base code to include CLPR functionality.

62281195
>>62281185
Yes. There is effort to be done. There is also effort to be done when a chain pays CLL to integrate CCIP. Why are those different? It's all just paying with either time or money to integrate with either CCIP or CLPR.

62281245
>>62281195
No chain is going to fundamentally change how they work for Hedera.
And Chainlink also works with cryptographic proofs end to end. Zero need to make L1 miners/validators/nodes/... pull double duty as oracles.

One of the oldest Chainlink fuds was "Ethereum will have native oracles". This CLPR shit is an even more retarded version of that.

62281268
>>62281245
This is not a Hedera thing.
Ethereum can talk with Solana with CLPR.

62281635
>>62275877
Not another LINK killer. It's over for like the 30th time

62281760
>>62275877
all the blocking they are doing shows how scared they are to lose retail funding. we need this token to crash to zero so the last 300m coins they have are worthless. this will light a fire under sergey azz. bankrupt them faster. force layoffs.

62283173
>>62283147
your pic doesn't matter because your cuckold ponzi scheme is trading at $8. Lol

62283180
>>62283173
you know what doesn't matter? all the shitcoins listed in that pic.
except for LINK of course

seethe

62283187
>>62283180
great for ChainlinkLabs, meaningless for cuckold baggies. Price + chart

62283372
>>62281268
>CLPR is not a Hedera thing
"CLPR (“clipper”) is a new bridgeless Cross-Ledger Protocol developed by Hashgraph"
https://hashgraph.com/clpr/

>>62283173
>fundamentals don't matter
So go to the casino and stop shitting up this board then.

62283678
>>62283372
>go to the casino
kek you mad

62283702
>>62283372
>"CLPR (“clipper”) is a new bridgeless Cross-Ledger Protocol developed by Hashgraph"
Yes but two blockchains such as Ethereum and Solana can use it without having to interact with Hedera at all.

62283715
>>62283702
>without having to interact with Hedera at all
Either Hedera "servers" interact with them ("step 1"), or they integrate Hedera's code into their base chain code ("step 2").

62283730
>>62283715
>Either Hedera "servers" interact with them ("step 1")
No the servers would be in their respective ledgers, not hedera.
> or they integrate Hedera's code into their base chain code ("step 2").
It's not "Hedera's code" whatever the fuck that means.

Two ledgers can talk with each others without having to interact with Hedera at all. Cope all you want it's just how it is.

62283741
>>62283730
>the servers would be in their respective ledgers, not hedera
They're Hedera's servers. The servers run Hedera's code.

>It's not "Hedera's code"
CLPR is coded by Hedera. It's Hedera's code.

62283782
>>62283741
Two ledgers can talk with each other via CLPR without having to interact with the Hedera DLT at all.

Keep coping jew.

62283798
>>62283782
>via CLPR without having to interact with the Hedera DLT at all.
Not talking about the DLT, I'm talking about the project.

62283808
>>62283798
So again, why are you so sure that CLPR will not be adopted at the validator level? This makes CCIP and all other bridges obsolete.

62283817
>>62283702
>>62283730
>>62283782
Why do you keep larping about the technicals when they havent been released?

62283819
>>62283808
Firstly because no other chain wants to alter their base code and functionality by injecting someone else's shit.
Secondly because it's retarded to ask blockchain validators/miners/nodes/... to moonlight as oracles.

62283824
>>62283817
Did you even read
https://hashgraph.com/blog/clpr-a-new-bridgeless-standard-for-cross-ledger-communication/
?
I know it's not in depth but it's something

62283873
>>62283824
yeah I screwed up, i meant the guy you were talking to and one other retard. the only rebuttal so far has been to throw as much bullshit at the wall as possible. i think this clpr thing is not getting the serious consideration from CLL it deserves.

62283889
>>62278023
lmao i didnt report shit lol
>>62278047
swapping ids? where faggot? i posted the op in my car, now im on wifi. made that very clear early on.

i get it you guys married your bags, but I didnt and until this is addressed by CLL im going to continue to assume there’s a serious contender until it is addressed by serg.

62283896
>>62283678
you people are so weird constantly id hopping, it gives away that you're jeets, nobody else would care that much on this dead board.
also to the other two faggots, anon engaged you seriously and you gave the game away by getting mad at him, you're not genuine link holders you're hbar baggies spamming the board. you faggots did this once before when link first joined that faggy council trying to act like friends and getting people to buy your bags. hbar is never going to be a thing here, leemon is never going to be a meme, go somewhere else (although i get you're paid to be here so it is what it is)

62283902
>>62283873
What I'm talking about is literally from the documentation.
In all cases, this system requires the introduction of external data from one chain to another.

Step 1 involves "dedicated servers", which basically means third party oracles.

Step 2 involves ledgers implementing CLPR "natively" whereby the validators become the endpoints.
Meaning the ledger's miners/nodes/validators/... have to operate as oracles.

62283908
>>62283873
>has been to throw as much bullshit at the wall as possible.
lmao that is exactly what you faggots are doing in this and like 20 other threads on the board right now. meanwhile anon actually rebuts everything and you faggots just pilpul and pretend you didn't hear previous responses.

62283922
>>62283896
>>62283908
>there can be NO questions
keep coping faggot. count my posts and suck these nuts.
>>62283902
ok then post the fucking sauce

62283946
>>62283922
>ok then post the fucking sauce
The sauce is all over this thread

62283951
>>62283922
you are an hbar baggie, you are a faggot, hbar will never ever be a thing

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 113 replies — 136.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 113 • Updated: 2026-05-28 14:06 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62278410
>>62276888
You just didnt make money on crypto

62278419
>>62276880
Retard. Stop smoking

62278837
>>62274703
How you can say it's legit?

62278840
>>62275501
can go to zero? In what way

62278849
>>62274705
Delusional joker

62278858
>>62273985
It had always been fishy.

62280044
>>62278837
You can literally verify everything yourself. We’re way past the point where major players like Aave, Ledger, GoMining, a16z and others would touch obvious garbage for no reason.

62280052
>>62272017
A lot of people still view the market through old cycle assumptions.

62280441
>>62271712
Bitcoin is a liquidity sink and will be rugged in a bug deflatinary swoop when the time is right.

62280858
>>62273653
problem with index funds is you just never make enough to make it unless you're like 20-30 years at it

62282426
>>62280858
cryoto is better than everything

62282600
>>62276888
you would've sold them at $10

62282845
>>62271712
I just inherited almost a million usd and I'd like to put 100,000 into bitcoin but I don't know how :(

I'm also very concerned about getting ripped off :(

I also don't want to have to deal with taxes :(

Will you help me? Is there a way to buy it and somehow store it offline or is it only accessible online? Do I have to make some account at Binance or some other site?

62282847
>>62271712

62282849
>>62280044
There are many similar offerings ended up nothing so it's scary

62282854
>>62282845
Send it to me

62282864
>>62282845
kys
see
>>62282847

62282885
>>62282864
I don't wanna kill myself though. I just became a millionaire! Life is finally getting good for me :)

I just wanna buy a bitcoin. Why won't you help me?

62283041
>>62272050
Hmmm.. what do you mean?

62283050
>>62282849
That’s why people should wait for the actual TBV launch first instead of screaming revolution or scam this early.
At least the Aave v4 governance process is already moving now.

62283055
>>62272643
At least check it sometimes bro. You might already be retired and just don’t know it yet.

62283065
>>62272208
A 2x or 3x from here would still outperform most traditional investments by a huge margin.

62283375
>>62283050
Defi hacks are no joke

62283377
>>62282845
Why are you flexing your imagination?

62283380
>>62271712
Money

62283769
>>62276880
ARMA hasn't passed yet, but there's already a Reserve.

62283901
THE BIG DUMPING
>every cycle 90-70% loss. why would it be this time different.

62283926
>>62283901
diminishing returns mean diminishing drawbacks, retard

62283940
>>62282847
I hope that big brain stuck around, for my sake

62283952
>>62271712
Bitcoin is a pyramid scheme and a waste of electricity. I was an early adopter of bitcoin fully thinking it was a scam and I sold mine in 2016 after I had enough to retire. I understand the whole point about fiat currency but what I think we need to do is just get rid of money all together and use assets as money like stocks and commodities. Money was invented so people didn't have to carry their assets around but know we all walk around with computers in our pocket that would allow us to trade our assets like how things used to be. I should be able to go to McDonalds and buy my food with corn for example. Bitcoin only exists for countries to bypass sanctions and if you buy bitcoin you are just giving more money to Putin and all the other scammers and terrorists.

item.title: why does this keep happening — 171 replies — 49.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
why does this keep happening

Open thread • Replies: 171 • Updated: 2026-05-28 11:27 UTC

OP

>Link pumps to $10
>BTC instantly takes a nosedive

Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?

Early replies (first 3)

62263473
because fuck you, stinkie
it really all boils down to that
the entire crypto market exists JUST TO FUCK YOU STINKIES OVER
how does that make you feel
it makes me feel pic related

62263519
>>62263473
hey it's the dick guy

62263588
But Sirgay said they've decoupled from crypto prices

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62271116
>>62271020
>And they don't give a shit about 4chan posters
you clearly do, which is why you are here calling people "schizos" for noticing BTC dumps whenever LINK gets positive news (pic rel). i dont spend a single minute discussing people in xrp threads or twitter: why would you post 26 times in this thread?

you have done this before havent you

62271138
>>62271104
>he didn't respond to my post so i'll give him another (you)
thanks cuck

>>62271106
>wordy and spammy
There's many posters and I'm trying to get each of you in the time window I have. It's slower to condense my thoughts.

>and what does this crystal do, desperate for pussy voice

>>62271116
>you clearly do
because im here all the time should i fren post you so you know
>26 times in this thread
you all keep responding and i'm conversing back
>you have done this before havent you
yeah i really like link and want good arguments for it

I'll just say this: I'm mainly hitting you all with points I could potentially see people using on your arguments here. I'm saving your incelsheets to regurgitate back if I feel like playing on your side next time and defend the idea. This back-and-forth helps me really hone in on solid points. If you actually said anything that made me genuinely question the prosecution side, I would use that the next time I defend until some new anon said another better point, and then i'd repeat the loop. In the end I'll have the best higher tier saying that is concise yet profound. You see this a lot with oldfag posts that say very little but a lot at the same time.

62271155
>>62271138
You got SCHOLED, kid.
You didn't respond because you got buckbroken from multiple angles.
You said:
>Why none from the 2017 era?
And got shown one from the 2017 era here >>62271074
You said:
>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
And got shown a lot from 2022-2023 here >>62271096
You said:
>or even the most recent link news?
And got shown one from 2025 here >>62271116

62271158
>>62271066
>>62271155
>or even the most recent link news

62271176
>>62271155
These all got posted AFTER i said "why none".
It does refute my point of all the screenshots being from the same era, but it doesn't disprove the point that it's bullshit victimizing. Also, your tone is triggered faggot. Tell me how I know our little chat is going to emotionally bother you for hours after our talk.

>>62271116
>>62271158
My response to these are going to be the same as it's been for the others. So what I'll do is watch and create the same format when this isn't true about link when news drops, and I believe we'll find many more examples of it not happening that otherwise would get ignored in favor of the emotionally charging dumps screenshot fest here. Sound fair?

62271202
When it does 10x-20x it will be quick

62271203
>>62271202
>Motley Fool
kek baggie you've been saying this for the past 6 years, enjoy reading your boomer rag

62271402
>>62271176
Now say that without crying.

62273954
>>62271176
Notice how every single "argument" you put forth was simply swept away with a relevant example of Link suppression.
Yet here you are, still trying to drown out reality with your wordswordswords.

You're going through the mother of all cognitive dissonances.

62274025
>>62269848
>A spiritual war between good and evil

Please shut the fuck up. You are gay. Muuh spirituals.

62277201
>>62273954
You're theories only make sense to 4chan posters with a persecution kink. Bring them outside this site and you're into heavy schizo territory.

62277225
>>62277201
Fallacy ad populum, faggot. You argue like a woman

62277275
Its weird checking in on this board like once a week and seeing the usual paid fud just never stops, and how much of it just never gets responded to or probably even seen
There's at least like 10-20k hours worth of collective fuddie manpower that would have made no difference if it had never existed at all. It would be tragic if the time of third world subhumans was worth anything to begin with

62277295
>>62277225
>the stuff we say is true because we can bias it into reality y...you're just wrong for disagreeing with our spam
Half this thread is top of the debate pyramid calling me retarded every other post. You faggots wonder why ironic shit posting is all that happens here.

62277481
>>62271176
You are Jewish as fuck.

62277668
>>62263230
Imagine telling people in 2019 that in 2026 people would be theorizing that LINK is being suppressed under $10 KEK

It was supposed to be $1000 by now

62277674
>>62271202
Bearish article paid for by link whales hunting for exit liquidity

62277803
>>62277295
>30pbtid

62278136
>>62277295
You tried to double down despite another anon posting like a dozen screencaps proving that link is specifically targeted by suppression, which is just a fraction of how many times it has actually happened. Link absolutely 100 percent is a suppressed asset
So yes. You are a retard. A useless, shit-eating brown retard who sucks at his "job" trying to gaslight people on a dead board.

62278171
you link fags have ruined this entire board with your schitzo nonsense. this is proof to me that having higher-than-average iq doesnt necessarily mean that you are 'smart' if you cant understand the greater context of why your coin is failing in the first place

62278428
>>62278171
>schitzo nonsense
This thread is full of objective, verifiable proof. It cannot be denied.
At the most you can claim that this is not exceptional and the same happens to all alts, but so far nobody has ever shown a shred of proof after years of making exactly this claim.

It's schizo to deny this is happening.

62279583
>>62277275
>fud
why do cuckolds use this word? Just check the chart

62279597
Looks like LINK suppression finally killed BTC

I wonder whats gonna happen next

62279623
>>62279597
mhh....your cuckold token crashes to $2 and misses another bullrun? Just a guess

62280508
>>62277275
Remember the Windows VR cooking spirit advertising with a VR world full of corpses?
https://www.breitbart.com/tech/2020/04/14/microsoft-publishes-then-takes-down-commercial-with-spirit-cooker-marina-abramovic/

Windows served as an intermediary in spiritual attacks during my sleep and I had to switch to linux to stop them.

I am starting to think that Chainlink becoming integrated into the financial world into every transaction would conquer darkness and insert light into every transaction.

>>62271066
How does it feel to serve evil and to deny light and god?
The result is always the same because darkness cannot exist without light as they are energy vampires.
So yes this planet can be entirely corrupted and light can leave it and everything will be saved that way but even you won't enjoy that experience because you will be their next sacrifice when they run out of babies to torture.

62280516
>>62264135
>buy into your secret club we are massively advertising
kek

62282935
>>62263230
>Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?
Too many people are holding the bags from years ago desperately waiting to dump. I call it the bag pressure.
Wikipedia will be listing bag holders as the death cause of Link in a few years

62282960
If your crypto isn't useful for buying drugs you're holding a shitcoin.
Seethe.

62283111
Crypto crashed again today, while the stock market had another green day at ATH.

If Bitcoin hadn't been used to dump on Link at every turn, it would have kept up with the stock market at the very least.

62283386
>>62269753
It’s gonna be a lot less than 10k to make it. It’s probably gonna be 500. There’s only 25,000 people with more than 1k link. I’m sure 90% with 10k over are 4chan. 4chan is an aberration. 5,000 or so mentally retards who found it long before anyone else.

The make it stack right now is probably very similar to what someone finding out about ETH at $10 could reasonably put in. 10k? 20k?

item.title: IT ALWAYS GOES UP! Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever! — 24 replies — 19.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT ALWAYS GOES UP! Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever!

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-05-28 13:41 UTC

OP

IT ALWAYS GOES UP!
Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever!

Early replies (first 3)

62283021
>>62283018
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

62283022
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

62283030
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283037
>>62283018
that's a really good point. I just buyed

62283047
now show other market index's like the FSTE 100, is it even above 2008 levels yet????

62283203
>>62283039
ONE nothing wrong with me
TWO

62283258
It's always misleading as fuck
>Your current holdings get rekt by 90%
>It's okay though the stuff you buy from this point onwards is cheap and will grow in recovery
>But what about everything I held before?
>Still rekt for years on end
>We cope by calling that "reducing your average buy in"
Not falling for it personally

62283283
>>62283018
Objective truth. Never doom.

62283382
so why should we be immune from a Japan style 30 year stagnation? We had one before 1960. Allowing retards like Trump in power is begging for it.

62283411
>>62283382
Then buy Chinese or Euro stocks, most of which have already been sideways for ~2 decades.

62283417
>>62283382
Japan largely stopped being innovative desu, not America’s case so far

62283444
>>62283411
Many famous Euro stocks are actually down 30 or 40% since 2000 lol (Mercedes-Benz, Shell, Deutsche Bank, Philips, Bouygues, UniCredit, Veolia…)

62283452
>>62283185
So you need to have over 30m now to have what a millionaire had 1900. That's why millionaire was really special back then. So a million now is not even 30k in 1900.

62283465
>>62283444
I'm also amazed at some Chinese ones. Vanke used to be the flagship of Chinese stock market and the most prestigious real estate developer. The ticker number for it was chosen to be 000002, after 000001 being the Shanghai Composite Index

62283472
>>62283452
That distinction still exists, we call them UHNWIs nowadays

62283495
>>62283465
>Vanke
Looks like a crypto after the rug pull, almost as bad as Vivendi and Orange (the absolute juggernauts of the French stock market in 2000, they were supposed to crush the world with all their ambitious tech… 26 years later, still 90% down after the bubble burst…)

62283519
>>62283018
So they're just flat-out admitting that inflation has been 10% every year since 1950?

62283598
>>62283039
Is this inflation adjusted?

62283626
>>62283598
Dividing SPX by the USM2 is almost the same thing as adjusting to inflation.

62283790
>>62283411
How do you know which stock market might be the next Nikkei? It went up, until it didn't, and then took 35 years to recover

62283830
>>62283790
>which stock market might be the next Nikkei?
All of them
